name: Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail
name_original: El Embalse de Guadalest – Circular desde Abdet
description: Cycle the Embalse de Guadalest – Circular from Abdet, a difficult 13.7-mile route with stunning valley views and the historic Guadalest vill
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Confrides are set within the Sierra Aitana mountains, offering a landscape characterized by dramatic mountainous terrain and prominent peaks. The region features winding roads through pine forests and valleys, alongside striking limestone formations. Cyclists can also encounter natural springs and the distinctive turquoise waters of the Guadalest Reservoir. Best touring cycling routes around Confrides The most popular touring cycling route is Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Guadalest Reservoir and Castell de Guadalest. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular from Quatretondeta, a difficult 22.5 miles (36.3 km) path. This route explores the Sierra de Almudaina, providing a challenging gravel experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Port de Tudons – Ascent Puerto de Tudons loop from Sella, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Confrides is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and limestone formations. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists, with 28 difficult routes and 4 moderate routes available. The routes in Confrides are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 25 reviews. More than 3,500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Confrides's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Confrides?
A: The routes around Confrides are primarily set within the dramatic Sierra Aitana mountains, featuring winding roads through fragrant pine forests and valleys. You'll encounter striking limestone formations, prominent peaks, and the distinctive turquoise waters of the Guadalest Reservoir. The region is known for its challenging slopes and stunning mountainscapes.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Confrides?
A: While Confrides is renowned for its challenging terrain, there are some routes that cater to varying fitness levels. The region offers 4 moderate routes, which might be more suitable for those with good fitness but less experience with very steep climbs. For a scenic and relatively accessible option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952600227/guadalest-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful views and a path around the reservoir.

Q: What are some of the most scenic touring cycling routes or viewpoints in the Confrides area?
A: Many routes offer spectacular views of the Sierra Aitana mountains and the Guadalest reservoir. The road between Raco del Fillet and El Castell de Guadalest (utilizing routes like CV-715 and CV-70) is particularly favored for its breathtaking scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952600227/guadalest-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views of the reservoir and Castell de Guadalest. You can also find a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reservoir viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; along the way.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Confrides?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Confrides are designed as circular loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952600227/guadalest-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31797684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons – Ascent Puerto de Tudons loop from Sella&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are the most challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Confrides is a cycling paradise for those seeking a challenge, with 28 difficult routes available. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952658099/the-sierra-de-almudaina-gravel-circular-from-quatretondeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular from Quatretondeta&lt;/a&gt; offer a demanding gravel experience with significant elevation gain. The ascents to mountain passes like Port de Confrides, Sa Creueta Pass, and Port de Tudons are also highly regarded for their challenging slopes and rewarding views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical attractions. You can cycle past the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; with its distinctive turquoise water, and catch glimpses of Castell de Guadalest. Other notable features include the ruins of Alfofra Castle and the traditional village of L'Abdet. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/191445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Winding Road Through Pine Forests (CV-715 and CV-70, Guadalest Valley)&lt;/a&gt; is an attraction in itself.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Confrides?
A: The touring cycling routes in Confrides are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 25 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning scenery, challenging climbs, and the excellent, smooth road surfaces, particularly on popular ascents like Port de Confrides. The region is frequently described as a 'cycling paradise' with some of the 'best cycling roads in Europe'.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes popular with touring cyclists in Confrides?
A: Yes, several mountain passes are very popular. &lt;strong&gt;Port de Confrides&lt;/strong&gt; is a well-known and enjoyable climb, offering gentle gradients for much of its ascent with a few steeper sections. &lt;strong&gt;Sa Creueta Pass&lt;/strong&gt; features gradients up to 15% on its west ramp and is known for its 'super asphalt'. &lt;strong&gt;Port de Tudons&lt;/strong&gt;, reaching over 1,000 meters, provides impressive views and a rewarding descent. You can explore a route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31628978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent Puerto de Tudons – Port de Tudons loop from Racó del Pas&lt;/a&gt; to experience one of these climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Confrides?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures for tackling the climbs. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, so early morning rides are advisable. Winter offers cooler temperatures, but the mountain passes are generally accessible.

Q: Are there any gravel touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy off-road cycling, there are options like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952658099/the-sierra-de-almudaina-gravel-circular-from-quatretondeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular from Quatretondeta&lt;/a&gt;. This route provides a challenging gravel experience through the Sierra de Almudaina, combining the region's stunning landscapes with a different type of cycling surface.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in total around Confrides?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Confrides. These include 4 moderate routes and 28 difficult routes, offering a wide range of options for cyclists of varying experience levels, with a strong emphasis on challenging mountain rides.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: Confrides is characterized by its mountainous terrain, so significant elevation gains are common. Routes often feature climbs over 500 meters, and some can exceed 1000 meters of ascent. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952658099/the-sierra-de-almudaina-gravel-circular-from-quatretondeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1100 meters of elevation gain.
tips:
Embassament de Guadalest, the Guadalest reservoir, is certainly one of the most famous reservoirs in the area from Torrevieja to Alicante. Thanks to the beautiful castle in Guadalest with the even more fantastic view of the reservoir, this lake is known to many people and is a very popular tour destination.

Excellent! The tour around the reservoir is a real highlight! 👌🚴‍♀️👏 The path leads around the entire reservoir, and the Castell Guadalest can be viewed from all sides. The route around the lake can either be started over the dam and followed the path along the water or the tour can be started in the mountain village of Beniardá.

An impressive experience with stunning views. The tour is well worth it! The view of Guadalest is one of the most breathtaking panoramic experiences in the Costa Blanca hinterland. The historic mountain village is spectacularly situated on a cliff high above the Guadalest River valley. From viewpoints in the surrounding area, a breathtaking panorama unfolds: the old houses cling to the steep rock face, the ruins of Guadalest Castle tower above, and below, the turquoise waters of the Guadalest Reservoir shimmer. This combination of rock, water, and medieval architecture makes the view of Guadalest one of the most beautiful photo opportunities in the Alicante region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route, covering approximately 22 kilometers, generally takes around 1 hour and 43 minutes to complete. This duration is for the cycling portion itself and does not include stops for sightseeing or breaks.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of The Embalse de Guadalest – Circular from Abdet route?
faqs.answer: Komoot categorizes this as a 'difficult' touring bicycle route, requiring 'very good fitness'. While the section directly circling the Guadalest Reservoir has no significant physical or technical difficulty and follows asphalted roads, the overall loop includes significant climbing with an elevation gain of over 500 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, with the section around the Guadalest Reservoir being entirely asphalted. While gradients in the broader Guadalest area are often gentle (10% or less), some parts of the route might require caution for road bikes due to varying surface conditions.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: Cyclists are treated to breathtaking scenery, including expansive views of the Guadalest valley, typical pine trees, and often glimpses of the Mediterranean Sea. You'll see the municipalities of Benimantell and Beniardá, the impressive Aixorta and Serella mountain ranges, and the historic village of Guadalest dramatically perched above the striking turquoise waters of the Guadalest Reservoir. Don't miss the Blick on Guadalest viewpoint.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Abdet?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Abdet. While specific parking details for cyclists are not provided, Abdet is a small village, and you should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the village to begin your circular tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Guadalest area is renowned among cyclists, with many professional and amateur teams using it for winter training. This suggests that cooler months, outside of the peak summer heat, might be ideal. The region offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year, but spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route around the Embalse de Guadalest from Abdet. The roads are publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and requirement for 'very good fitness', this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. While the section directly around the reservoir is less challenging, the overall loop includes significant climbing and is better suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You will find it overlaps with parts of 'Callosa d'En Sarrià - Confrides pel riu' (59% of the route), '[CIMA AT04] Confrides (puerto de Ares)' (40%), and 'Guadalest - Cumbre de Aixortà' (21%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near the historic village of Guadalest, which is a popular tourist destination. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities there. Additionally, the starting point of Abdet may offer some local options.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a touring bicycle route primarily on paved roads, bringing a dog would typically involve them being in a trailer or basket. The route itself doesn't have specific restrictions against dogs, but it's not a traditional hiking trail where dogs would walk alongside you.
